displayTitle: 2025-10-15 - Transport and Infrastructure Committee - Petition of New Zealand Equestrian Advocacy Network: Vulnerable road user status for New Zealand horse riders
topic: Petition of New Zealand Equestrian Advocacy Network: Vulnerable road user status for New Zealand horse riders
description: 16 October 2025 | Transport and Infrastructure Committee Petition of New Zealand Equestrian Advocacy Network: Vulnerable road user status for New Zealand horse riders Ministry of Transport Paul O’Connell, Deputy Chief Executive, Sector Strategy Joanna Heard, Manager, Safety Amber Fraser-Mackenzie, Adviser, Safety New Zealand Transport Agency Anna Cleary, Acting National Manager, System Policy Vanessa Browne, National Manager, Strategy
descriptionSummary: Petition of New Zealand Equestrian Advocacy Network: Vulnerable road user status for New Zealand horse riders. Ministry of Transport. Paul O’Connell, Deputy Chief Executive, Sector Strategy

chunks.text: All right. Well look, welcome everyone. Um, thank you for coming along. Uh, included in that is a change to the passing distances around, uh, this is pedestrians, uh, cyclists, uh, that's due to be going out, uh, for decisions. We're hoping to get decisions June of next year. Um, and it's based on the accessible streets, uh, piece of work.

So there's 35 questions and we've got a bank and they're selected at random for each test. Um, and thankfully there is about a 98% pass rate for that question, which is one of our highest overall.
